 {"id":"bd-lxky","title":"Unit tests: animation subsystem determinism + reduce-motion","description":"Add deterministic unit tests for the demo_showcase animation subsystem.

Why:
- The showcase will lean heavily on motion; we need confidence it won’t become flaky or timing-dependent.
- CI must be able to validate animation code paths without real sleeps.

Test coverage:
- Given a fixed dt (e.g., 16ms) and fixed initial/target values, stepping the animation yields deterministic values.
- Animations stay bounded (no NaNs, no runaway values, no panics on extreme dt).
- Reduce-motion / `--no-animations` behavior:
  - animated values immediately snap to target (or the subsystem becomes a no-op) according to the design.
  - no tick scheduling when animations are disabled.

Acceptance:
- Tests do not depend on wall-clock time.
- Failures clearly report which animation invariant broke.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.665155320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:21:52.514623411Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:21:52.514494150Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.688967780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.679805544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lxky","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:35.684774144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-miqo","title":"E2E scenario: docs render + search + resize reflow","description":"Scenario: docs page rendering quality.

Steps:
- Open Docs
- Switch between two docs
- Search for a term
- Simulate resize (send WindowSizeMsg) and verify reflow occurs

Assertions:
- Rendered output changes with width.
- Search navigation finds matches.

Acceptance:
- Catches regressions in glamour integration and responsive layout.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:39.583157581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:15:35.847324443Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:15:35.847193920Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-2lkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:34.234331367Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:32.891451084Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-39bb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:35.534642717Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-miqo","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:39.598959258Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mwty","title":"Logs: implement live log viewer (viewport + follow mode)","description":"Implement the Logs page core UI (the thing users will stare at for minutes).

Why:
- Logs are the heartbeat of an ops console.
- This is where styling + performance + interaction quality are most obvious.

Requirements:
- Viewport that can scroll back through a large buffer.
- Follow mode (tail -f) toggle:
  - when enabled, viewport stays pinned to latest entries
  - when user scrolls up, follow pauses automatically
  - a clear UI hint indicates "paused" vs "following"
- Selection (optional but great):
  - highlight current line for copy/export

Performance:
- Must handle thousands of lines without flicker.
- Avoid rebuilding huge strings unnecessarily; prefer rendering only the visible window.

Integration:
- Uses structured log entries + formatter (`Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log`).
- Filtering/search is layered on later but the core viewer should support it cleanly.

Acceptance:
- Smooth scrolling and no flicker.
- Follow mode behavior matches user expectations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:36.085412578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:53:36.090025763Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:53:36.089960261Z","close_reason":"Implemented LogsPage with live log viewer: viewport scrolling, follow mode (tail -f), auto-pause on scroll up, status bar with level counts and position. Added 8 tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logs","viewport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mwty","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:33.789901378Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mwty","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:36.112421707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-mwty","depends_on_id":"bd-32pp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:36.416170919Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-ndn1","title":"EPIC: Property-Based Testing (proptest)","description":"Property-based testing using proptest for algorithmic and numeric code.

## Philosophy
- Let the computer find edge cases humans miss
- Test invariants, not specific inputs
- Shrink failing cases to minimal reproductions
- Focus on numeric/algorithmic code

## Target Areas
1. harmonica - spring physics calculations
   - Animation value convergence
   - Velocity damping behavior
   - Frame timing consistency

2. lipgloss - dimension calculations
   - Width/height calculations with padding/margin
   - Position calculations
   - Unicode width handling

3. bubbles - component state machines
   - Viewport scroll bounds
   - Progress bar percentages
   - Spinner frame cycling

4. glamour - text wrapping
   - Word wrap boundaries
   - Unicode character handling
   - ANSI sequence preservation

## Property Examples
- For any input, width >= content width
- Animation always converges to target
- Scroll position always in valid range
- Progress percentage always 0-100","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:06.367379778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:59:12.009040062Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ndn1","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:06.381903246Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":25,"issue_id":"bd-ndn1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PROPERTY TESTING STRATEGY:

Property tests complement unit tests by finding edge cases that humans miss.
They're especially valuable for:
- Numeric/mathematical code (harmonica springs)
- Dimension calculations (lipgloss widths)
- State machines (bubbles components)
- Text processing (glamour wrapping)

PROPTEST PATTERNS:

1. INVARIANT PROPERTIES - 'This must ALWAYS be true'
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn progress_always_valid(pct in any::<f64>()) {
           let p = Progress::new().set_percent(pct);
           assert!(p.percent() >= 0.0 && p.percent() <= 1.0);
       }
   }
   ```

2. ROUND-TRIP PROPERTIES - 'Encode then decode equals original'
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn style_render_preserves_content(s in "[a-z ]{0,50}") {
           let styled = Style::new().render(&s);
           let stripped = strip_ansi(&styled);
           assert_eq!(stripped.trim(), s.trim());
       }
   }
   ```

3. COMPARISON PROPERTIES - 'This equals that'
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn batch_order_independent(cmds in vec(any_cmd(), 0..10)) {
           // Results should be same regardless of batch order
       }
   }
   ```

SHRINKING: Let proptest shrink failures to minimal reproducible cases.
Always save the seed for reproducibility in CI.","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:59:12Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-ndn1","title":"EPIC: Property-Based Testing (proptest)","description":"Property-based testing using proptest for algorithmic and numeric code.

## Philosophy
- Let the computer find edge cases humans miss
- Test invariants, not specific inputs
- Shrink failing cases to minimal reproductions
- Focus on numeric/algorithmic code

## Target Areas
1. harmonica - spring physics calculations
   - Animation value convergence
   - Velocity damping behavior
   - Frame timing consistency

2. lipgloss - dimension calculations
   - Width/height calculations with padding/margin
   - Position calculations
   - Unicode width handling

3. bubbles - component state machines
   - Viewport scroll bounds
   - Progress bar percentages
   - Spinner frame cycling

4. glamour - text wrapping
   - Word wrap boundaries
   - Unicode character handling
   - ANSI sequence preservation

## Property Examples
- For any input, width >= content width
- Animation always converges to target
- Scroll position always in valid range
- Progress percentage always 0-100","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:06.367379778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T02:24:04.664864006Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T02:24:04.664793775Z","close_reason":"Completed: All child property test tasks closed (bd-11zs, bd-adw0, bd-q0nj, bd-71ze, bd-10x1, bd-dj49, bd-ygt5). Property tests now cover lipgloss, harmonica, huh, bubbletea, glamour, and bubbles.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ndn1","depends_on_id":"bd-665u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:06.381903246Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":25,"issue_id":"bd-ndn1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PROPERTY TESTING STRATEGY:

Property tests complement unit tests by finding edge cases that humans miss.
They're especially valuable for:
- Numeric/mathematical code (harmonica springs)
- Dimension calculations (lipgloss widths)
- State machines (bubbles components)
- Text processing (glamour wrapping)

PROPTEST PATTERNS:

1. INVARIANT PROPERTIES - 'This must ALWAYS be true'
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn progress_always_valid(pct in any::<f64>()) {
           let p = Progress::new().set_percent(pct);
           assert!(p.percent() >= 0.0 && p.percent() <= 1.0);
       }
   }
   ```

2. ROUND-TRIP PROPERTIES - 'Encode then decode equals original'
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn style_render_preserves_content(s in "[a-z ]{0,50}") {
           let styled = Style::new().render(&s);
           let stripped = strip_ansi(&styled);
           assert_eq!(stripped.trim(), s.trim());
       }
   }
   ```

3. COMPARISON PROPERTIES - 'This equals that'
   ```rust
   proptest! {
       #[test]
       fn batch_order_independent(cmds in vec(any_cmd(), 0..10)) {
           // Results should be same regardless of batch order
       }
   }
   ```

SHRINKING: Let proptest shrink failures to minimal reproducible cases.
Always save the seed for reproducibility in CI.","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:59:12Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-nw3u","title":"Fix run_conformance JSON timestamp (use real RFC3339)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T21:44:06.445942217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:58:47.358436941Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:58:47.358359595Z","close_reason":"Fixed: chrono_lite_timestamp() now uses chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339_opts() for real RFC3339 timestamps","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-o39u","title":"Integration Tests: bubbletea + bubbles","description":"Integration tests for bubbles components within bubbletea event loop.

## Purpose
Verify that bubble components work correctly in the full TUI context,
not just in isolation.

## Test Requirements

### Component in Model
- [ ] TextInput receives and handles key events
- [ ] Viewport scrolls with mouse wheel
- [ ] Spinner animates with ticks
- [ ] Progress updates from async commands

### Focus Management
- [ ] Tab navigation between components
- [ ] Focus styling changes correctly
- [ ] Blur handlers fire appropriately

### Command Integration
- [ ] Component commands execute via batch
- [ ] Timer ticks reach components
- [ ] Mouse events route to correct component

### Rendering Pipeline
- [ ] Component view() in parent view()
- [ ] Style composition doesn't corrupt
- [ ] Nested component rendering

## Test Approach
Use bubbletea simulator with multi-component Models.
Verify full update/view cycle for realistic scenarios.

## Success Criteria
- Components work identically in isolation and integration
- No event routing bugs
- No rendering artifacts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderMeadow","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:24.855009868Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T02:16:07.996456623Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T02:16:07.996385751Z","close_reason":"Complete: Added 5 new integration tests (scenarios 4-8) covering viewport mouse wheel scrolling, progress async updates, mouse event routing to components, nested component rendering with style composition, and focus styling changes. All 9 tests pass. Fixed existing tests (scenarios 1-3) that had broken API usage.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","bubbletea","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o39u","depends_on_id":"bd-1u1s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:21.687839434Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-o39u","depends_on_id":"bd-3nyf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:24.877440549Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-o39u","depends_on_id":"bd-9sjn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:24.205474766Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-pfqw","title":"demo_showcase: e2e test harness + scripts","description":"Build comprehensive end-to-end verification for the demo_showcase binary.

Why:
- The showcase is the "integration demo" for the whole workspace; it must be reliably testable.
- We want confidence that *everything still works* after refactors.

Constraints:
- E2E must run in CI without a real TTY.
- Avoid flaky sleeps/timeouts.

Strategy:
- Use bubbletea custom I/O + deterministic tick driving.
- Use a scripted input runner to navigate the UI and trigger actions.
- Capture output frames and structured logs as artifacts.

Definition of done:
- `cargo test -p demo_showcase` runs E2E scenarios quickly (<~10s) and deterministically.
- On failure, logs clearly explain which scenario/step failed and why.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:28.143197913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:36:06.350545439Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:36:06.350453899Z","close_reason":"Verified complete: test_support.rs (4,799 LOC) implements comprehensive e2e harness with ProgramSimulator, scripted navigation, deterministic tick driving. E2E scenarios cover smoke tour, wizard→jobs→logs correlation, settings/theme switching, file picker, logs export, mouse interactions. All 802 tests pass deterministically. Run in CI without real TTY via custom I/O.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pfqw","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:28.237574673Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-y3c8","title":"demo_showcase data: background simulation engine (tick/every)","description":"Implement a background simulation that updates data over time.

Use:
- bubbletea tick/every commands (and async equivalents if enabled) to drive state changes.

Testability requirement (critical):
- The simulation must be drivable *without real sleeps*.
  - Prefer a `Tick` message that carries `Instant`/frame count.
  - In production, schedule ticks with `tick/every`.
  - In unit/e2e tests, inject `Tick` messages directly (ProgramSimulator / custom IO) to advance time deterministically.

Behaviors:
- Jobs progress and complete/fail.
- New log lines appear.
- Service health flaps occasionally.
- Deployments start/finish.

Acceptance:
- UI feels alive and realistic without being chaotic; changes drive visible UI updates.
- Tests can advance the simulation 1000 ticks in milliseconds.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:51.780149859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:03:35.524175692Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:03:35.524111703Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-y3c8","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:51.796613145Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-y3c8","depends_on_id":"bd-3bz7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:49.460322408Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-y3c8","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:50.740656518Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-yghg","title":"Animations: loading states + micro-interactions","description":"Add micro-interactions and loading states that make the demo feel alive and premium.

Why:
- Small motion + state feedback is what separates a toy TUI from a "real product".
- This also stress-tests the animation subsystem and tick scheduling.

Examples to implement (pick a few that fit the UI):
- Loading spinners + skeleton/placeholder content for:
  - Docs loading
  - Jobs list refresh
  - File preview loading
- Subtle animations:
  - toast slide-in/out
  - progress easing
  - pulsing warning indicator for degraded service

Constraints:
- Must respect reduce-motion / `--no-animations`:
  - replace motion with instant state changes and clear static indicators
- Must not increase CPU usage unreasonably.

Acceptance:
- Loading states feel intentional and beautiful.
- No-animations mode still communicates state clearly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:59.479257020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:07:27.954086140Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:07:27.954019365Z","close_reason":"Implemented loading state components module with: LoadingSpinner (themed animated spinner with label, 5 style variants), SkeletonLine/SkeletonBlock (placeholder content with optional pulse animation), LoadingOverlay (centered loading screen), PulsingIndicator (attention-grabbing warning pulse). All components respect --no-animations and REDUCE_MOTION. 20 unit tests passing. Page-level integration is ready for individual page beads.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["anim","demo_showcase","spinner"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yghg","depends_on_id":"bd-2fnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:59.534704063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yghg","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:08.114572604Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-yghg","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:09.336208750Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-ygt5","title":"Property Tests: bubbles component state machines","description":"Property-based tests for bubbles component state invariants.

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify state machine invariants hold
for all possible input sequences.

## Properties to Test

### Viewport
- [ ] scroll_position always in [0, max_scroll]
- [ ] visible_lines <= content_lines
- [ ] viewport_top + visible_lines <= content_lines

### Progress
- [ ] percentage always in [0.0, 1.0]
- [ ] set_percent clamps out-of-range values
- [ ] width never negative

### TextInput
- [ ] cursor_position in [0, content_length]
- [ ] cursor never beyond content
- [ ] delete at position 0 is no-op
- [ ] insert increases length by 1

### Spinner
- [ ] frame_index always in [0, frame_count)
- [ ] tick advances by 1 (mod frame_count)
- [ ] custom frames work same as built-in

### List
- [ ] selected_index in [0, items.len()) or None
- [ ] visible_items <= items.len()
- [ ] filter matches subset of items

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn viewport_scroll_stays_bounded(
        content_lines in 1usize..1000,
        viewport_height in 1usize..100,
        scroll_ops in prop::collection::vec(0i32..10, 0..50),
    ) {
        let mut vp = Viewport::new(content_lines, viewport_height);
        for op in scroll_ops {
            vp.scroll(op);
            assert!(vp.scroll_position() <= vp.max_scroll());
        }
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- State invariants always hold
- No panic for any input sequence
- Edge cases (empty, max) handled","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderMeadow","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:21.619524500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T02:17:18.743180822Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ygt5","depends_on_id":"bd-9sjn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:59.683652437Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ygt5","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:21.653068222Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify state machine invariants hold
for all possible input sequences.

## Properties to Test

### Viewport
- [ ] scroll_position always in [0, max_scroll]
- [ ] visible_lines <= content_lines
- [ ] viewport_top + visible_lines <= content_lines

### Progress
- [ ] percentage always in [0.0, 1.0]
- [ ] set_percent clamps out-of-range values
- [ ] width never negative

### TextInput
- [ ] cursor_position in [0, content_length]
- [ ] cursor never beyond content
- [ ] delete at position 0 is no-op
- [ ] insert increases length by 1

### Spinner
- [ ] frame_index always in [0, frame_count)
- [ ] tick advances by 1 (mod frame_count)
- [ ] custom frames work same as built-in

### List
- [ ] selected_index in [0, items.len()) or None
- [ ] visible_items <= items.len()
- [ ] filter matches subset of items

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn viewport_scroll_stays_bounded(
        content_lines in 1usize..1000,
        viewport_height in 1usize..100,
        scroll_ops in prop::collection::vec(0i32..10, 0..50),
    ) {
        let mut vp = Viewport::new(content_lines, viewport_height);
        for op in scroll_ops {
            vp.scroll(op);
            assert!(vp.scroll_position() <= vp.max_scroll());
        }
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- State invariants always hold
- No panic for any input sequence
- Edge cases (empty, max) handled","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderMeadow","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:21.619524500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T02:22:34.314218925Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T02:22:34.314150307Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 23 property tests covering Viewport, Progress, TextInput, Spinner, and List components. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ygt5","depends_on_id":"bd-9sjn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:59.683652437Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ygt5","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:21.653068222Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ykth","title":"Demo Showcase: eliminate RwLock poison risk across all pages","description":"# Demo Showcase RwLock Poison Safety

## Problem
The demo_showcase pages use std::sync::RwLock for interior mutability in the
view() method (which takes &self per the bubbletea Model trait). There are 50+
locations across 4 page files that call .write().unwrap() or .read().unwrap():

- pages/logs.rs: ~26 locations (viewport, formatted_content, needs_reformat, last_dims)
- pages/docs.rs: ~46 locations (viewport, rendered_content, needs_render, last_dims, last_theme)
- pages/files.rs: ~25 locations (preview_viewport, last_width, last_theme)
- pages/dashboard.rs: ~6 locations (card_bounds)

If ANY thread panics while holding a write lock, the RwLock becomes "poisoned".
All subsequent .read().unwrap() and .write().unwrap() calls on that lock will
panic, cascading the failure through the entire application.

## Why It Matters
- In SSH mode (wish crate), each SSH session runs its own App instance, but
  the pages share RwLock-protected state within each instance
- The bubbletea event loop catches panics from update() but NOT from view()
  (view is called in the render path which is synchronous)
- A single out-of-bounds access or failed assertion in any page's update()
  while holding a write lock = permanent crash for that session
- This is especially dangerous because view() is called on every frame (60 FPS)

## Solution
Create a helper module or extension trait that wraps RwLock access with poison
recovery. Two approaches:

**Approach A: Extension trait on RwLock** (Recommended)
    use std::sync::RwLock;

    pub trait RwLockExt<T> {
        fn read_or_recover(&self) -> std::sync::RwLockReadGuard<'_, T>;
        fn write_or_recover(&self) -> std::sync::RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T>;
    }

    impl<T> RwLockExt<T> for RwLock<T> {
        fn read_or_recover(&self) -> std::sync::RwLockReadGuard<'_, T> {
            self.read().unwrap_or_else(|poisoned| {
                tracing::warn!("RwLock poisoned, recovering");
                poisoned.into_inner()
            })
        }
        fn write_or_recover(&self) -> std::sync::RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T> {
            self.write().unwrap_or_else(|poisoned| {
                tracing::warn!("RwLock poisoned, recovering");
                poisoned.into_inner()
            })
        }
    }

**Approach B: parking_lot::RwLock**
Replace std::sync::RwLock with parking_lot::RwLock which never poisons. This
is simpler but adds a dependency. parking_lot is widely used and well-tested.
It also has better performance characteristics (no syscall overhead on
uncontended locks).

Recommendation: Approach B (parking_lot) if adding a dependency is acceptable,
otherwise Approach A. Either way, the change is mechanical: replace all
.read().unwrap() and .write().unwrap() with the poison-safe equivalent.

## Key Files
- crates/demo_showcase/src/pages/logs.rs
- crates/demo_showcase/src/pages/docs.rs
- crates/demo_showcase/src/pages/files.rs
- crates/demo_showcase/src/pages/dashboard.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero .read().unwrap() or .write().unwrap() on RwLock in non-test demo_showcase code
- Poisoned locks recover gracefully (log warning, continue operating)
- All 838 demo_showcase tests pass
- SSH mode sessions survive panics in individual page operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:36.814344802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:23:10.783134414Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T01:23:10.783064223Z","close_reason":"All subtasks complete: bd-3v3x (create mechanism - parking_lot), bd-2ey1 (dashboard.rs), bd-2s30 (logs.rs), bd-3mwa (docs.rs), bd-2x95 (files.rs), bd-xnq2 (unit tests). All pages migrated to parking_lot::RwLock, no .unwrap() calls on locks.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ykth","depends_on_id":"bd-1s5p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:38:36.837669140Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":36,"issue_id":"bd-ykth","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETION CRITERIA: This work stream is complete when ALL subtasks are done:

 {"id":"bd-yqh4","title":"demo_showcase: flagship 'turned up to 11' showcase binary","description":"Create a new standalone Rust binary crate (`crates/demo_showcase`) that acts as the *flagship* demo app for the whole charmed_rust ecosystem.

Why:
- Prove end-to-end functionality across crates in one realistic, cohesive application.
- Provide a visually stunning, highly polished reference implementation ("how to build real apps with this stack").
- Serve as a regression surface: if the demo stays amazing, the library is healthy.

What this demo must showcase (integrated, not toy snippets):
- bubbletea: model/update/view architecture, commands, timers, resize handling, mouse events, focus, paste, async mode (feature-gated), graceful exit/terminal restore.
- lipgloss: layout (join/place), borders, width/height, alignment, wrapping, truncation, theming + live theme switching.
- bubbles: multiple components working together (viewport, list, table, progress, spinner, textinput/textarea, filepicker, paginator, timer/stopwatch, help).
- glamour: markdown rendering with themes + syntax highlighting (when enabled), width-aware wrapping, link formatting.
- huh: real form flows (validation, multi-step, confirm/select) embedded as an in-app workflow.
- wish (optional runtime mode): run the same app over SSH (or a subset) to prove SSH viability.
- charmed_log: styled logging output and/or an in-app log viewer.
- harmonica: animations/transitions (numbers, progress easing, panel transitions).

Definition of done (high level):
- `cargo run -p demo_showcase` launches a coherent app with multiple pages/panels and a beautiful theme.
- Every major crate feature above is exercised in a way that looks like a real-world product.
- Documented run instructions + keybindings; no panics; terminal state restored on exit.

Notes:
- Prefer a single cohesive "product" story (e.g., ops console / project dashboard) over a grab-bag.
- Use a strong theming system so everything feels unified.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:55.126209979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:35:05.063848802Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:35:05.063752613Z","close_reason":"Verified complete: demo_showcase (49k+ LOC) is a fully functional flagship demo showcasing all charmed_rust crates - bubbletea (App model, commands, timers, mouse, resize), lipgloss (theming, borders, layout), bubbles (16 components in use), glamour (Docs page), huh (Wizard forms), wish (SSH mode), charmed_log (Logs page), harmonica (Animator). 802 tests pass, cargo run works, terminal properly restored.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","flagship","showcase"]}
